servinfo.title: Rear Shock Absorber Inspection

ptxt.intxrefpoff: Rear Shock Absorber Removal and Installation

attention1.ptxt: Since high-pressure gas is sealed inside rear shock absorber (1), there is risk of personal injury if it is handled without observing the following precautions.

item.ptxt: Do not disassemble rear shock absorber.
Do not put rear shock absorber into fire.
Do not store rear shock absorber where it gets hot.

Before disposing of rear shock absorber, be sure to drill an approximately 3 mm (0.1 in.) diameter hole (2) in it at point indicated by the arrow in the figure to let gas out of rear shock absorber together with oil. Be sure to wear eye shield when drilling hole because metal chips produced by drilling can injure your eyes although the gas itself is harmless.
Inspect rear shock absorber for deformation or damage.
Inspect rear shock absorber upper bushing for wear or damage.
Inspect rear shock absorber for evidence of oil leakage.
Inspect rear shock absorber for deterioration.

servinfo.ptxt: If check result is not OK, replace rear shock absorber with new one.
